text: User: also let's make sure we have a builder doc explaining how to make a nip60 app using NMP -- to start -- give me the very very high level of how a nip60 wallet app would work with NMP [...] **The builder doc is now tracked**: [NMP #2872](https://github.com/pablof7z/nostr-multi-platform/issues/2872), also added as a checklist item under Phase 1 of epic #2864. The issue pins down the doc's shape (the two-things-only model, two walked-through nutzap flows), its constraints (ADR-0075: zero Trellis vocabulary; no secrets in examples; capability-gating as the canonical pattern), and its timing — draftable today against the design doc's Product Surface section, but validated and landed with Phase 1, since that's when the real `nmp.wallet.*` action names and projection schema exist. Writing it before Phase 5 matters: Chirp's wallet UX should be buildable *from the doc*, which is the cheapest possible test that the doc is right. [...] That's the whole builder experience: **actions in, projection out**. Everything with a private key, a proof, a mint URL decision, or a relay decision is on the Rust side of the line — which is also why the builder doc must never mention Cashu internals as things the app manipulates (and per ADR-0075, never mention Trellis at all).
